description: iOS push notification fix for delivery partner alerts and heading in location tracking
steps: 1. Update public/sw.js push event handler: Ensure the notification options object includes requireInteraction: true (always, not conditional on payload), renotify: true, and a unique tag per order (already has tag: payload.orderId ? `order-${payload.orderId}` : 'new-order' - this is good but add renotify: true). Set requireInteraction to true unconditionally in the options object (currently it checks payload.requireInteraction || false - change to always true for delivery alerts, or better: always pass it through from payload but default to true).

2. Update the push notification payload sent to delivery partners in app/api/admin/orders/[id]/delivery/route.ts: The sendPushToUser call already passes requireInteraction: true. Confirm this is correct. The payload object should also include orderId so the service worker can use it for the tag. Verify the sendPushToUser in lib/push.ts passes through the orderId field in the JSON payload.

3. Update lib/push.ts sendPushToUser: Ensure the JSON payload stringified and sent to the browser includes the orderId field (it currently passes the full payload object - verify this includes orderId).

4. Update components/delivery/delivery-orders-client.tsx location tracking useEffect: In the watchPosition callback, include position.coords.heading in the POST body to /api/delivery/location. The field should be heading: position.coords.heading ?? null (heading can be null if device doesn't provide it). The schema in lib/validations.ts already accepts heading as optional, and the API route already stores it (heading: parsed.data.heading ?? null). The DeliveryMap component already uses the heading prop for arrow rotation. So only the client-side POST body needs updating.

5. Verify the full chain: delivery-orders-client.tsx posts heading -> /api/delivery/location stores it in DeliveryLocationEvent -> live-order-tracking.tsx SSE stream should include heading in deliveryPartnerLocation (check app/api/orders/[id]/stream/ route if it exists, or the poll endpoint that provides location data to the customer tracking view).
acceptance_criteria: public/sw.js push handler sets requireInteraction: true and renotify: true in notification options
The heading field is included in the location POST body from delivery-orders-client.tsx
next build completes without TypeScript errors
verification: Run npx next build to confirm no TypeScript/build errors
Run npx tsx --test tests/*.test.ts to confirm existing tests still pass
findings: Pre-existing test failure in scroll-preservation.test.ts (test 'root elements allow native scrolling') unrelated to this change. User model does not have currentHeading field, so heading is fetched from the latest DeliveryLocationEvent for the order in both stream and tracking routes.
